seat_matrix: per phase: 3x gpt-5.5-fast xhigh (correctness, docs-vs-code, regression) + 1x MiMo v2.5 Pro (fresh-eyes) + 1x Fable 5 xhigh (adversarial synthesis); 24 phases x 5 = 120 seats
verification: every strong P1 cluster (Fable-flagged or 2+ seat corroborated) re-verified by refute-first Fable 5 seats with file:line proof

confirmed.title: Changelog mislabels restart durability as REQ-003
confirmed.proof: changelog-013:60 ('Durability across a restart mid-repair is the deferred REQ-003 work, shipped in phase 020') is materially false: spec.md:111 defines REQ-003 as the KNN scalar-JOIN vs vec0-MATCH live-corpus benchmark with an evidence-pending deferral blocked by daemon E040, and phase 020's spec/implementation-summary contain no KNN benchmark content; the line-33 claim 'read queries respect the degraded/quarantined shard state' is also false — vector-index-queries.ts has zero degraded/quarantine references (only sqlite-vec availability checks at lines 363/506/1523).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Reword the follow-up to attribute restart-mid-repair durability to phase 020 without the REQ-003 label (REQ-003's live-corpus KNN rerun remains deferred), and fix line 33 to describe the actual change (KNN query-shape benchmark helpers).

confirmed.title: Staged-shard rename over live attachment orphans writes
confirmed.proof: reindex.ts:613-623 unlinks sidecars and renameSync's the staged shard over activeShardPath without detaching, on the same live connection the repair was started with (vector-index-store.ts:703 passes `db: database` into startVectorShardRepairReindex → enqueueJob → runJob); the follow-up attachActiveVectorShard (reindex.ts:667-669) compares only the PRAGMA database_list path string (vector-index-store.ts:456-458) so it takes the path-equality branch at vector-index-store.ts:1501, and the attached-shard probe (lines 492-507: quick_check + table existence) passes against the stale healthy inode, keeping the attachment — subsequent writes via the temp aliases land in the unlinked old inode and are lost on restart.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Checkpoint and DETACH active_vec before the staging rename and re-attach after, or make attachActiveVectorShard compare on-disk vs attached inode (fs.statSync ino) and force detach/re-attach on mismatch.

confirmed.title: Changelog byte-identical ranking parity claim
confirmed.proof: changelog-014:23 and :44 claim ranking was 'byte-identical to legacy', but tests/bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts:231-233 asserts the engines rank differently by design (legacyTop !== '101', packedTop === '101'), runBM25EngineComparison only gates packedAtLeastLegacy/titleSignalRestored (bm25-baseline.ts:228-236), and the phase's own evidence records packed MRR@5 1.0000 vs legacy 0.9000 (implementation-summary.md:105) — a materially false shipped verification claim.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Replace 'byte-identical' with the actual proven gate: packed meets-or-beats legacy on all baseline metrics and restores the title/trigger signal; only warmed-vs-direct packed parity is identical.

confirmed.title: Repudiated 111 MB RSS still shown as P0 PASS evidence
confirmed.proof: implementation-summary.md:103 still records 'Current corpus budget fixture | PASS ... RSS spike 111,017,984 bytes' and line 116 says 'The current corpus gate passed, so the minisearch contingency is not triggered', while the same packet's spec.md:139 explicitly repudiates that measurement (stop-word fixture, realistic re-validation 686.8 MB breach, 'the RAM gate test is left failing and the contingency decision fires') and changelog-014:46 records 'Warmup RSS | OVER BUDGET at this phase' — the impl summary contradicts the phase's own current record.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Replace the budget-fixture PASS row and line-116 limitation with the realistic-fixture breach evidence plus the phase-017 closure pointer, and reconcile the continuity block (no blockers, completion_pct 100) with spec.md's re-opened contingency.

confirmed.title: Impl summary claims RSS gate passed after phase recorded failure
confirmed.proof: Same defect as the previous finding: implementation-summary.md:103 (PASS at 111,017,984 bytes) vs spec.md:139 (686.8 MB breach, RAM gate test left failing, contingency fired) and changelog-014:46 (OVER BUDGET) — duplicate seat reports of one confirmed doc-truth defect.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Single fix shared with the previous finding: reconcile implementation-summary.md's verification table, limitations, and continuity with spec.md and the changelog.

confirmed.title: Repair de-dup lost across restart
confirmed.proof: reindex.ts:124 vectorShardRepairJobs is module-local and only populated at job creation (742-744); resumeReindexJobs (777-793) re-enqueues persisted queued/running jobs without rebuilding it, and startVectorShardRepairReindex (809-822) de-dupes only by scanning that map, so after a crash mid-repair the sentinel path (vector-index-store.ts:677-684 → 1231-1245 → 700-712) schedules a second same-shard repair alongside the resumed job — violating the phase's shipped de-dup claim (spec.md:109, implementation-summary.md:71) with no Known-Limitations entry covering restart (implementation-summary.md:129-135).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Persist repair reason/shardPath with the embedder_jobs row (or side table), rebuild the map in resumeReindexJobs, and have the guard query persisted queued/running same-shard repairs before inserting.

confirmed.title: filePath fallback tags human saves as 'import'
confirmed.proof: write-provenance.ts:90-93 maps any untagged write whose path word-matches import|ingest|scan|index to 'import'; memory-save.ts:2272-2276 always threads filePath and a plain memory_save passes no provenance labels (3529-3531, tool='memory_save' matches no label regex), real spec folders match the regex (e.g. 027/003-memory-index-causal-lifecycle, 026/.../012-memory-index-scan-ux-hardening), and memory-crud-update.ts:88-91 protects only null/'human'/constitutional rows — so the heuristic strips manual-overwrite protection while genuine scan/ingest writers already tag explicitly (memory-save.ts:2858-2862), directly contradicting the changelog's 'Default safety PASS: an untagged write derives human, never automated' (changelog line 46).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Delete the filePath branch from deriveSourceKindFromContext so untagged writes truly default to 'human', and rely on the explicit provenanceSource tags the real scan/ingest paths already set.

confirmed.title: Status Complete with 4 of 5 handoff gates never run
confirmed.proof: implementation-summary.md:46 says Status Complete (Completed 2026-06-11) while its own Verification table lines 118-121 lists tsc, the provenance/guard suite, validate.sh --strict, and comment-hygiene as 'Pending final run' (continuity completion_pct 95, next_safe_action 'Record final ... results after they run'); checklist.md CHK-021/022/023 are unchecked 'To be filled after final run', yet spec.md:46 says Complete with completion_pct 100 and the changelog verification table claims those suites PASS — three shipped docs make irreconcilable completion claims against the handoff criteria at spec.md:52.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Run the four gates and record real results in the verification table and checklist evidence rows, or downgrade spec/summary Status until they are recorded.

confirmed.title: Changelog overstates guard enforcement
confirmed.proof: changelog-022 frontmatter line 3 and summary line 23 claim 'the write-ingress guard enforces the constitutional rule that automated writers never overwrite manual or constitutional memory' / 'the guard now holds the line', while the phase's own spec.md:237-241 and implementation-summary.md:143-144 document P0-class same-path-retire and auto-promotion paths where automated writers can still overwrite manual rows, and spec.md phase context states the phase 'does not expand guard enforcement beyond tagging' — the unqualified enforcement claim is materially false despite the trailing follow-ons sentence.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Rewrite frontmatter/summary to claim provenance tagging plus existing memory_update guard coverage only, explicitly naming same-path retire and auto-promotion enforcement as open follow-ons.

confirmed.title: Impl-summary documents pre-remediation state
confirmed.proof: Commit 76cf460eaa shipped BOTH the 12-test suite (commit message: 'flag-ON suite 12/12 (was 10)', adding the won/lost store contract, lookupIdempotencyReceiptByKey, and the 'signals won/lost on store so a concurrent loser replays the winner by key' test at vitest line 122) AND implementation-summary.md:104 claiming 'PASS ... 10 tests passed' with What Was Built (lines 53-72) listing only three fixes — the canonical completion record is materially stale relative to the code in its own commit.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Patch the verification table to the 12/12 result and add the fourth remediation (won/lost store contract + lookupIdempotencyReceiptByKey concurrent-loser replay) to What Was Built.

confirmed.title: Secret scrubber covers index artifacts only; raw secret persisted to canonical markdown
confirmed.proof: memory-parser.ts:276-281 scrubs only the parsed/index copy, while the same save request persists raw input: memory-save.ts:3705-3707 falls back to raw currentContent (written via writePendingFile/promotePendingFile at 3772-3778), and the canonical routed path feeds raw params.content into buildCanonicalMergePayload (memory-save.ts:1664-1670), whose 'append-section' branch embeds it as body text (1429) into a second, server-authored durable doc — contradicting implementation-summary.md:72 ('so secrets never reach durable storage') and checked-off CHK-061 (checklist.md:111 claims body coverage); the scrubbed alternative (preparedMemory.parsed.content) exists and is even used when quality-loop fixes apply (runQualityLoop runs on parsed.content at memory-save.ts:518), making the raw-persist branches an inconsistency, not a convention — mitigation: the non-canonical branch only re-writes bytes already in the agent-authored source file (content is read from disk at memory-save.ts:3490), so the live contract breach is the canonical merge copying raw secrets into the target doc.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Scrub once at the head of atomicSaveMemory (fail-closed on SecretScrubberError) and feed the scrubbed string to both the merge payload (use preparedMemory.parsed.content instead of params.content) and the persistedContent fallback.

confirmed.title: memory_update provenance metadata bypasses the secret scrubber
confirmed.proof: scrubSecrets has exactly two call sites repo-wide (memory-parser.ts:276, memory-crud-update.ts:173-177) — provenance strings are never scrubbed: memory-crud-update.ts:325 persists getInternalProvenanceContext(args) raw, and write-provenance.ts:111-152 writes provenance_source/provenance_actor verbatim into memory_index, breaching spec.md:273 ('provenance strings (not content-only)') and CHK-061 which is checked [x] with evidence that never mentions provenance (checklist.md:111); while the cited memory_update vector is internal-only on default config (strict memoryUpdateSchema at tool-input-schemas.ts:300-307 rejects __provenanceContext), the identical unscrubbed sink is externally reachable TODAY via memory_save's allowlisted provenanceSource/provenanceActor params (tool-input-schemas.ts:610) flowing through applyWriteProvenance at handlers/save/create-record.ts:368-373.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Scrub provenanceSource/provenanceActor (and other persisted provenance strings) fail-closed inside buildPostInsertProvenanceFields in write-provenance.ts so every sink (memory_update, memory_save, index scan, auto-promotion) is covered at one choke point.

confirmed.title: Low-level memory delete can commit after causal tombstone sweep failure
confirmed.proof: vector-index-mutations.ts:149-151 swallows every sweepCausalEdges error before delete_memory_from_database commits 'DELETE FROM memory_index' at :692 inside the same transaction (:660) — the catch's 'legacy databases' rationale is already handled inside sweep.ts:225-227 (missing causal_edges returns emptySweepResult), and the repo's own contract at causal-edges.ts:864-866 ('Let errors propagate so callers inside transactions see failures... previously swallowed') is exactly what this path violates; with no SAVEPOINTs (transaction-manager.ts:261), a mid-sweep failure can even commit partial tombstones while leaving active orphan edges and a deleted memory row.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Drop (or narrow to nothing) the catch around sweepCausalEdges in deleteAncillaryMemoryRows so sweep/tombstone failures propagate and roll back the surrounding delete transaction; the missing-table no-op already lives inside sweepCausalEdges.

confirmed.title: Direct tombstone sweeps do not bump the causal-edge generation cache key
confirmed.proof: sweep.ts:211-222 invalidateGraphCaches clears only degree/graph-signal caches while the generation counter is bumped solely in causal-edges.ts:201-225 (documented at :190-195 as 'bumped on every causal-edge mutation'), and memory-search.ts:952-987 keys the tool cache on getCausalEdgesGeneration() with causal boost default-ON (search-flags.ts:48-50) and the 60s tool cache default-ON (tool-cache.ts:65-66); the checkpoint-restore path is reachable today and unmitigated — handlers/checkpoints.ts:549/563 sweeps edges via checkpoints.ts:1645-1660 but clears only the legacy query_cache (vector-index-aliases.ts:231) and never runs runPostMutationHooks, so stale causal-boosted memory_search payloads survive under unchanged keys (corrections.ts:191/620-665 has the same gap at library level; only the MCP delete handlers are saved by memory-crud-delete.ts:289 → invalidateOnWrite).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Move bumpCausalEdgesGeneration into sweep.ts's shared invalidation (or a single exported causal-graph invalidator used by both sweepCausalEdges and causal-edges.ts), and add a regression asserting a direct sweep changes getCausalEdgesGeneration().

confirmed.title: Shadow stats via console.info pollute stdout MCP JSON-RPC channel
confirmed.proof: memory-triggers.ts:569 uses console.info (stdout in Node) while context-server.ts:2179 runs StdioServerTransport where stdout carries JSON-RPC frames; rg confirms no console.log/info reassignment anywhere in mcp_server, and every other runtime log in the same handler uses stderr-bound console.warn/error (lines 557, 582, 624), so when SPECKIT_SEMANTIC_TRIGGERS=true in the default shadow mode (memory-triggers.ts:182-186) — the phase's documented handoff criterion 'shadow telemetry recorded' (004-tests-goldens-shadow-eval/spec.md:49) — every weak-lexical trigger call injects a non-JSON inspect blob into the protocol channel, breaking the MCP stdio framing contract.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Change console.info at memory-triggers.ts:569 to console.error so shadow telemetry goes to stderr like every other server log.

confirmed.title: Failed maintenance runs are not recorded as last-run errors
confirmed.proof: memory-embedding-reconcile.ts:79-89 records recordMaintenanceRun only with status:'success' while all failure paths (lines 23-35, 47-59, 97-122) return without recording, memory-retention-sweep.ts:53-62 vs its catch at 88-110 has the identical gap, and rg confirms no caller in the codebase ever records status:'error' even though the module's own contract defines it (retrieval-observability.ts:60, 294-296); memory_health exposes the snapshot unconditionally (memory-crud-health.ts:863), so on a default configuration a failed reconcile/sweep leaves memory_health reporting the previous success as the 'last run', breaking REQ-005/SC-004 (008 spec.md:126, 138) and the impl-summary claim that operators see 'what the latest maintenance passes counted' (implementation-summary.md:57), with no documented deferral (known limitations cover only process-restart reset, implementation-summary.md:121).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Call recordMaintenanceRun with status:'error' plus sanitized error metadata in every early-abort and catch path of the scan/reconcile/retention handlers.

confirmed.title: Restore panel markdown bypasses the restored/omitted budget
confirmed.proof: resume-ladder.ts:393-398 renders renderContinuityFacets(facets) — the full unbudgeted facet set — while the budgeted restored/omitted selection computed at resume-ladder.ts:362-377 is used only for the counts; the markdown flows unconditionally (no flag gate) into the default startup payload via session-resume.ts:649 and session-bootstrap.ts:358-364, breaking the documented REQ-001 contract (spec.md:119 'Restore output respects a fixed budget'); markdown also omits the 'Recovered <doc>' document candidates counted as restored, so counts contradict rendered content in both directions; tests (openltm-continuity-resilience.vitest.ts:128-135) assert only counts and headings, never that omitted text is absent.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Render markdown from the budget-selected restored items grouped by facet (or filter facets to the restored set before renderContinuityFacets) and add a regression test asserting an over-budget item is absent from restorePanel.markdown.

confirmed.title: Opt-in PreCompact snapshot wipes authored continuity fields
confirmed.proof: authored-continuity-snapshot.ts:124-126 hardcodes completion_pct: 0 and empty open/answered_questions and never sets session_dedup; mergeFrontmatterWithContinuity (thin-continuity-record.ts:913-929) replaces the entire _memory.continuity mapping with buildOrderedRecord(record, false), and buildOrderedRecord:837 only emits session_dedup when present on the new record — so an authored completion_pct (test fixture has 50 at openltm-continuity-resilience.vitest.ts:93), the session_dedup fingerprint that SPECKIT_COMPLETION_FRESHNESS validates, and question state are all destroyed on every enabled PreCompact; though flag-gated (default off), this breaks the documented REQ-002 contract (spec.md:120 'summarizes existing state') and is irreversible data loss in the authored source-of-truth docs.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Read the existing thin continuity record from implementation-summary.md and merge, preserving completion_pct, session_dedup, open_questions, and answered_questions unless the ladder supplies explicit new values.

confirmed.title: PreCompact spec-folder detection turns spec doc file paths into nonexistent folders
confirmed.proof: compact-inject.ts:116 regex /\.opencode\/specs\/[\w/-]+/g excludes dots, so '.../implementation-summary.md' is captured as '.../implementation-summary'; the strip at compact-inject.ts:123 requires a '.ext' suffix in the captured match and never fires, and the bogus path is passed at compact-inject.ts:403 to refreshAuthoredContinuitySnapshot, whose resolveSpecFolderPath (authored-continuity-snapshot.ts:157-167) skips with 'Resolved spec folder does not exist' — defeating documented SC-002 for the common file-mention transcript shape when enabled, and the same bug feeds a wrong 'Active spec folder' line into the default merged compaction context at compact-inject.ts:246 (dotted track dirs like specs/anobel.com break entirely).
confirmed.fix_sketch: Allow dots in the candidate match (or capture to whitespace/quote boundary), strip known file components after matching, and add a compact-inject test with an implementation-summary.md transcript path.

confirmed.title: Restore panel renders unbudgeted facet content after computing omissions
confirmed.proof: Same defect as the first finding group: resume-ladder.ts:393-398 assembles markdown from the unfiltered facets while the 8-item/1200-char budget (resume-ladder.ts:120-121, applied at 362-377) shapes only the structured restored/omitted arrays, so over-budget facet text still renders in the user-facing panel while omittedCount claims it was not restored.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Build a filtered ContinuityFacets from the restored selection before calling renderContinuityFacets, and enforce maxChars on the final markdown.

confirmed.title: Authored snapshot refresh erases continuity completion and question state
confirmed.proof: Same defect as the snapshot finding above: buildContinuityRecord (authored-continuity-snapshot.ts:109-128) emits a zeroed/empty record that upsertThinContinuityInMarkdown writes via full-mapping replacement (thin-continuity-record.ts:921-928 spreads frontmatter but overwrites continuity wholesale), so a completed packet's authored completion_pct, question state, and session_dedup are regressed to 0/empty/absent on every enabled refresh.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Preserve existing continuity fields by reading the current record and merging rather than rebuilding from ladder defaults.

confirmed.title: Claude/Codex orchestrator templates point at .opencode agent definitions
confirmed.proof: .claude/agents/orchestrate.md:19 mandates '.claude/agents/*.md' as the only runtime path and :162 requires .claude/agents/code.md be loaded, yet the mandatory Task Format at :195, the PDR example at :277, and the anti-pattern text near :820 all instruct loading .opencode/agents/<name>.md; .codex/agents/orchestrate.toml has the identical contradiction (:24 '.codex/agents/*.toml' vs :200, :282, ~:825), while the .opencode canonical is internally consistent — the mirrors were runtime-adapted at the convention/guard/table lines but missed in the dispatch templates, so following the mandatory template in Claude/Codex loads the wrong runtime mirror and contradicts the file's own consistency guard today, with no flag gating.
confirmed.fix_sketch: In the mirror conversion, render the Task Format, PDR example, and anti-pattern paths as the runtime-local directory (.claude/agents/<name>.md, .codex/agents/<name>.toml) instead of hard-coded .opencode paths.

confirmed.title: Canonical save lock can expire during a live save
confirmed.proof: generate-context.ts:57 sets CANONICAL_SAVE_STALE_MS=30_000 and acquireCanonicalSaveLock:415-423 reaps any lock whose directory mtime exceeds it — the owner file written at :409/:421 records a PID that is never read back, and nothing refreshes the lock mtime — while :786-806 shows the lock brackets the entire runWorkflow (full canonical save including embedding generation and doc regeneration), which can plausibly exceed 30s, so a second save started during a long live save reaps the active lock and runs concurrently against the same canonical files; this is the default, unflagged save path and no packet doc documents the 30s reap as an accepted policy.
confirmed.fix_sketch: Check the recorded owner PID for liveness (and/or heartbeat-refresh the lock mtime during the workflow) before reaping, and fail closed when the owner cannot be proven dead.

refuted.title: memory_retention_sweep handler drops feedbackRetention options
refuted.proof: Nothing is silently dropped: the published MCP contract declares dryRun as the only input with additionalProperties:false (tool-schemas.ts:499-503), and the strict-by-default zod schema (tool-input-schemas.ts:28-31, 332-334, allowlist at 618) loudly rejects feedbackRetention as an unrecognized key before the handler runs; the narrow surface is intentional defense — the same validation layer declares feedback signals 'system-generated only' (tool-input-schemas.ts:667-698), so forwarding caller-supplied signals/shadowEvaluationPassed as the suggested fix proposes would open a feedback-spoofing surface, and the feature is opt-in default-off anyway (memory-retention-sweep.ts:408 isFeedbackRetentionLearningEnabled) with the blocked active gate failing safe (audit-only, no deletions).

refuted.title: Receipt replay never sets the promised replayed marker
refuted.proof: The verbatim replay is the documented intended behavior, not a defect: changelog/023-idempotency-flag-on-correctness/changelog-023-idempotency-flag-on-correctness.md:23,36 explicitly records 'Replay returns the original response verbatim instead of stamping a replayed:true marker — a replayed call is indistinguishable from the first call, which is the point of idempotency', and before-vs-after.md:71 repeats it; the seats cite the older 007/002 spec and changelog promise that 023 deliberately superseded; the only residue is stale doc drift in feature_catalog/02--mutation/memory-idempotency-receipts-and-near-duplicate-hints.md:27 and manual_testing_playbook/19--feature-flag-reference/memory-idempotency-replay-and-conflict.md:20,48, which still describe the retired replayed:true contract — a doc-sync chore, not a runtime defect.

downgraded_p2.title: Auto setup config path vs artifact_dir
downgraded_p2.proof: deep_start-review-loop_presentation.md:19 does instruct a flat `{spec_folder}/review/deep-review-config.json` write and lines 247/252 claim child phases always use `{packet}-pt-{NN}/`, contradicting the flat-first resolver (review-research-paths.cjs:267-274 returns flat for a child-phase first run); however the auto YAML independently resolves artifact_dir (deep_start-review-loop_auto.yaml:127) and writes the canonical config at `{artifact_dir}/deep-review-config.json` via step_create_config (state_paths line 92), so on default first-run/root configurations the flat path and artifact_dir coincide and runtime behavior is unchanged — this is a stale presentation contract (also deep_start-context-loop_presentation.md:118), not a runtime defect.

downgraded_p2.title: Bench gate asserts mean, SC-002 names p95
downgraded_p2.proof: causal-traversal-bfs-equivalence.vitest.ts:264-265 gates `bfs.mean <= cte.mean` while spec.md:119 states SC-002 as p95-at-or-below-CTE — the mismatch is real in current source; but the gate is opt-in (SPECKIT_BENCH_GATE=1, advisory warn by default at line 266-268) and implementation-summary.md:84,124,130 already discloses that the wall-clock comparison is advisory and p95 overlaps within noise, so there is no default-config runtime impact; gpt-regression's P1 overstates an opt-in path.

downgraded_p2.title: Comparison helper ignores configured NDCG cutoff
downgraded_p2.proof: bm25-baseline.ts:267 hardcodes computeNDCG(...,10) while runComparisonMetrics receives k (line 244) and the doc comments promise k applies to NDCG (lines 43, 'NDCG at configured K' on the ndcg10 field) — real code-vs-doc mismatch; but both engines are measured at the same cutoff so packed-vs-legacy comparisons are not skewed between engines, the shipped evidence is correctly labeled 'NDCG@10' (implementation-summary.md:105), and the field is explicitly named ndcg10 — an eval-harness API doc inconsistency, not a P1; mimo's 'skews comparisons' overstates.

downgraded_p2.title: Read-only embedding load runs schema-ensure helpers
downgraded_p2.proof: skill-graph-db.ts:1242-1245 does call ensureVecTableForDim/hasActiveEmbedderPointer (DDL in embedders/schema.ts:57-101), but empirically CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS on a readonly better-sqlite3 handle is a no-op when the table exists and only throws when it is missing — and the sole caller catches that throw and degrades to an empty lane with a disabledReason (semantic-shadow.ts:195-207), which is exactly the graceful degradation getDbReadOnly's contract (skill-graph-db.ts:404-411) requires; remaining issue is the comment-vs-code mismatch at line 1236 and an imprecise disabled reason, not a runtime break.

downgraded_p2.title: Lost-store conflict falls through to divergent success
downgraded_p2.proof: The code gap is real — memory-save.ts:3611-3619 handles only winner.status==='replay' and falls through to `return response` at 3625 while lookupIdempotencyReceiptByKey can return 'conflict' (idempotency-receipts.ts:136-137) — but the entire path is gated on SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY which is default-OFF (idempotency-receipts.ts:54-57), the sequential conflict contract REQ-004 is enforced and tested pre-save (memory-save.ts:3469-3479), and hitting the gap needs a concurrent miss/miss race with identical fingerprint but different payload after the loser's mutation already persisted — no default-configuration runtime impact today.

downgraded_p2.title: Duplicate source dedupe can defeat same-query causal preference (+ no blank-id gate)
downgraded_p2.proof: Both logic flaws are reproducible in source — session-trace-causal-reducer.ts:113-116 dedupes per memory_id by newest timestamp ignoring query_id before the same-query preference sort at :121-126, so a later different-query sighting displaces same-query evidence under the 5-source cap; and :233-263 copies memory ids into insertEdge with no blank-id guard while insertEdge (causal-edges.ts:304-341) only rejects self-loops/non-finite strength and feedback_events.memory_id permits empty strings (feedback-ledger.ts:135, no CHECK) — but SPECKIT_SESSION_TRACE_CAUSAL_INFERENCE defaults OFF (ENV_REFERENCE.md:92), the reducer noops with 'flag_off' at :187-189, and grep finds zero production call sites (module header :5: 'No live feedback logging path imports or invokes this module'), so nothing changes on a default configuration today.

downgraded_p2.title: Empty trigger responses drop semantic-trigger telemetry
downgraded_p2.proof: Code-true — memory-triggers.ts:585-608 returns createMCPEmptyResponse without the semanticTriggerShadow/semanticTriggerUnion extraMeta that only the success path attaches at 783-789 — but the flag is default-off, shadow stats are still emitted via the (spec-documented) log path at line 569 which runs before the empty branch, the phase contract documents shadow telemetry as logging not response annotation ('log would-have-fired hits', 004-tests-goldens-shadow-eval/spec.md:97) with the cold-start no_query_embedding signal asserted at matcher level (trigger-cold-start.vitest.ts:59-61), and union mode is documented as blocked pending promotion evidence (parent spec.md:66), so no default-config behavior or documented contract breaks today.

downgraded_p2.title: Trace observability can go stale on memory_search cache hits
downgraded_p2.proof: Code-true — memory-search.ts:1000-1001 returns the cached payload, vectorDegradation is computed only on the fresh path at 1249-1252 from isVectorSearchAvailable() at build time, and cache args at 957-988 carry no vector-health generation while the cache is on by default with a 60s TTL (tool-cache.ts:65-66) — but the staleness is bounded to one deliberate 60s cache window whose key design consciously handles invalidation (causal-edges generation comment at 947-954), the cached trace faithfully describes the retrieval that produced the cached results rather than silently returning lexical-only results, and REQ-004's other surface memory_health computes the degraded-vector signal live on every call (memory-crud-health.ts:862), so this is bounded diagnostics staleness, not a P1 correctness break.

downgraded_p2.title: Completion-claim detection misses Status table / evidence markers
downgraded_p2.proof: The gap is real — continuity-freshness.ts:114,128 reads status only from YAML frontmatter and :221-223 only matches literal 'Evidence:' while templates/manifest/spec.md.tmpl puts Status in the metadata table and validation_rules.md:522-525 documents [Test:]/[File:]/[Source:]/[Commit:] markers — but the rule is default-off (validate.sh:831,1046 gate on SPECKIT_COMPLETION_FRESHNESS), warn-only without ENFORCE, fails only in the skip/false-negative direction consistent with the phase's explicit 'must not over-block / no-false-positive' constraint (006-peck-verification-discipline/spec.md), and the canonical save flow stamps completion_pct:100 in continuity frontmatter which line 226 does detect, so the standard completion path is covered today.

downgraded_p2.title: memory_update receipt key never varies per logical update (fable)
downgraded_p2.proof: The defect logic reproduces exactly: memory-crud-update.ts:225-228 fingerprints only {id, contentHash}, idempotency-receipts.ts:90-97 excludes the payload from the receipt key, vector-index-mutations.ts updateMemory sets only updated_at/title/trigger_phrases/importance_weight/importance_tier (never content_hash), :542-544 stores a receipt after the first success, no code deletes from memory_idempotency_receipts, and the :250 recovery hint 'submit a fresh logical update' is structurally unsatisfiable — but SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY is default-off and changelog-023 explicitly documents the flag as staying off pending a deliberate enablement decision with enablement gating (force-retry-conflict, receipt TTL) named as remaining work, so it changes no runtime behavior today on a default configuration; the update-key gap is a real pre-enablement blocker that should be added to that 023 follow-up worklist.

downgraded_p2.title: memory_update idempotency blocks later metadata updates (gpt seats)
downgraded_p2.proof: Same defect as the fable finding and equally reproducible (memory-crud-update.ts:221-237 key-vs-payload split, idempotency-receipts.ts:136-137 conflict on changed payload_hash, :243-253 fail-closed response): a second distinct metadata update on an unchanged-content memory derives the same receipt key and is rejected as a conflict — but it is gated behind default-off SPECKIT_MEMORY_IDEMPOTENCY, which changelog-023-idempotency-flag-on-correctness.md documents as remaining off pending enablement gating, so no default-configuration behavior changes today; downgrade to a pre-enablement correctness item.
